qa_pairs.question: What is the command used to compile `main.cxx` adhering to the C++11 standard without explicitly specifying the Application Binary Interface (ABI)?
qa_pairs.answer: The command `$(CXX) -c -o main-cxx11.o -std=c++11 $<` is used to compile `main.cxx` into `main-cxx11.o` using the C++11 standard with the default ABI.

qa_pairs.question: How is `main.cxx` compiled to specifically use the old Application Binary Interface (ABI) when targeting the C++11 standard?
qa_pairs.answer: To compile `main.cxx` using the old ABI with the C++11 standard, the command `$(CXX) -c -o main-cxx11-oldabi.o -std=c++11 -D_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=0 $<` is used.

qa_pairs.question: Which compiler option is used to explicitly enable the new C++11 Application Binary Interface (ABI) when compiling `main.cxx` with the C++11 standard?
qa_pairs.answer: The option `-D_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=1` is used to explicitly enable the new C++11 ABI, as shown in the command `$(CXX) -c -o main-cxx11-newabi.o -std=c++11 -D_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=1 $<`.
